data.title: Republican Senate leader still sees chance government won't shut down - report
data.description:
A government shutdown is still "avoidable," Sen. John Thune (R-SD) said, even though President Donald Trump said the government will shut down "if it has to."

The federal government's funding ends on Sept. 30, the last day of its fiscal year.

A Republican measure to fund the government through Nov. 21 failed to pass as Democrats refused to endorse it. Rather, the Democrats are seeking to reverse Medicaid cuts made in Trump's massive tax and spending bill passed earlier this year and to extend subsidies for Affordable Care Act premiums.

“I’m a big believer that there’s always a way out,” Thune said in an interview with The Associated Press. “And I think there are off-ramps here, but I don’t think that the negotiating position, at least at the moment, that the Democrats are trying to exert here is going to get you there.”

In comments he made to reporters at the White House on Friday, Trump said, "If it has to shut down, it'll have to shut down," according to Bloomberg News.

Earlier this week, Trump canceled a meeting with Democratic leaders, saying he saw no possibility of a productive meeting. On Thursday, the White House told federal agencies to prepare for mass firings if the government shuts down on Oct. 1.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Anthropic to ramp up global expansion in enterprise AI
data.description:
Anthropic, the generative artificial intelligence company backed by Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) and others, said on Friday it will ramp its global expansion in enterprise AI.

As part of the push, Chris Ciauri is joining Anthropic as its Managing Director of International, the company said in a statement. Ciauri previously served as CEO of Unily, and held executive positions at Google Cloud, and Salesforce.

Anthropic said the push for international expansion comes as Claude usage is largely outside the U.S.

“Anthropic’s recent Economic Index report found that nearly 80 percent of consumer Claude usage comes from outside the United States, with per-capita usage in countries like South Korea, Australia, and Singapore outpacing that of America’s,” the company wrote in a statement.

Anthropic added that it owns the top spot in enterprise AI (citing data from Menlo Ventures), as its revenue run-rate surpassed $5B last month, up from $87 at the start of 2024. Anthropic now has more than 300,00 global customer bases, up from less than 1,000 business customers two years ago.

As part of the global expansion, Anthropic will add more than 100 new roles in its Dublin and London offices and its Zurich office, with added European locations to come. The company also said it would be opening its first Asian office in Tokyo, Japan and bringing further offices to the continent “throughout the coming year.”

“The global demand for Claude is extraordinary—from financial services in London to manufacturing in Tokyo, enterprises are trusting Claude to power their mission-critical operations,” said Ciauri. “This is a key moment for Anthropic to expand the infrastructure and partnerships needed to serve this growing international customer base.”

Earlier this month, Anthropic raised $13B in a Series F funding round that valued the maker of its Claude chatbot at $183B.

data.title: Crocs bank on Sydney Sweeney to give HEYDUDE sales a lift with new ad campaign for Austin Lift
data.description:
Crocs (NASDAQ:CROX) is betting on Sydney Sweeney’s star power by naming the popular actress the new face of its Austin Lift shoes from its HEYDUDE collection.  

A new ad campaign featuring Sweeney in Austin Lift shoes has given shares of parent company Crocs (NASDAQ:CROX) a boost in Friday’s trading.

 

 

Crocs (NASDAQ:CROX) is betting on the success of Sweeney’s campaign with American Eagle (AEO) to lift sales for its struggling HEYDUDE product line as the label reported another 3.9% drop in sales in the latest quarter following a 10% drop in Q1.

The brand has struggled to gain momentum among Gen Z and Millennials who have called the open-laced HEYDUDE shoes akin to “wearing a Kleenex box.” The loose fit, high price, and quirky style have failed to gain traction, and coupled with brand mismanagement by Crocs (NASDAQ:CROX), disappointed investors.

But by capitalizing on the popularity of clogs and with the help of Sweeney, Austin Lift shoes are aiming for a demographic looking for a trendier version of the Birkenstock (BIRK) Boston at a more affordable price ($79 for the Austin Flat vs $170 for the Birkenstock Boston).

Sweeney’s ability to move jeans could translate into success for the clunky Austin Flat. According to American Eagle -- and despite controversy surrounding the ad campaign -- Sweeny Signature jeans sold out within a week, and some products sold out within a day.

“She is a winner and in just 6 weeks, the campaign has generated unprecedented new customer acquisition…the momentum is national, and it is pervasive,” said American Eagle’s (AEO) Chief Marketing Officer Craig Brommers said in the company's latest earnings call.

data.title: Alkermes upgraded at RBC Capital Markets ahead of key catalyst
data.description:
Alkermes (NASDAQ:ALKS) shares spiked on Friday after RBC Capital Markets upgraded the Irish biotech to Outperform from Sector Perform, citing a compelling buying opportunity ahead of a key late-stage trial readout for the company’s sleep therapy, alixorexton.

RBC analyst Leonid Timashev highlighted the stock’s decline from the day Alkermes (NASDAQ:ALKS) reported mid-stage trial data for the once-daily oral therapy against narcolepsy type 1 (NT1), a sleep disorder.

“Despite the negative reaction (-7% since the presentation) to the NT1 data, we think it demonstrated that alixorexton has reproducible and clinically meaningful efficacy in NT1, and a tolerable profile, taking major safety risks off the table,” Timashev wrote.

With Alkermes (NASDAQ:ALKS) set to disclose mid-stage data for the drug this fall from its Vibrance-2 study in patients with narcolepsy type 2, the analyst raising his price target to $44 from $42 per share indicated a buying opportunity.

“Given the sell-off post NT1 data, and our belief that sentiment continues to be bearish on NT2 and ALKS's potential market positioning, we see a buying opportunity into the NT2 data,” Timashev wrote.

data.title: Gold Fields completes Gold Road takeover, sells Northern Star stake for A$1.1B
data.description:
Gold Fields (NYSE:GFI) +3.1% in Friday's trading after finalizing its A$3.3 billion (~US$2.4 billion) purchase of Gold Road Resources (OTCPK:ELKMF), allowing it to take 100% ownership of the Gruyere gold mine in Western Australia, and agreeing to a A$1.1 billion (US$725 million) sale of shares in Northern Star Resources (OTCPK:NESRF) held by Gold Road.

The Gold Road (OTCPK:ELKMF) takeover became legally effective following court orders lodged with the Australian Securities and Investments Commission.

The Gruyere mine, which Gold Fields (NYSE:GFI) has operated in a joint venture with Gold Road (OTCPK:ELKMF), is expected to produce 325,000-350,000 oz of gold this year.

Gold Fields (GFI) said proceeds from the Northern Star (OTCPK:NESRF) sale will be used to repay part of the bridge facility that will be drawn down to fund the Gold Road (OTCPK:ELKMF) takeover.

Gold Fields (GFI) shares have tripled YTD, riding a record-breaking rally in gold that saw prices hit another all-time high this week.

data.title: Sanuwave refinances debt with new credit facility
data.description:
• Sanuwave Health (NASDAQ:SNWV) has announced the refinancing of its $27.5M debt facility with a $23.0M new secured credit facility from J.P. Morgan, 1.0M withdrawn from a two-year $5.0M revolving credit facility, and the remainder was repaid using cash accumulated from the company’s operations and from its sale of certain intravascular shockwave patents.
  • The $23.0M loan will amortize over a four-year period, and a two-year $5.0M receivables are backed by a revolving credit facility.
  • The new credit facility will have an interest rate of SOFR (Secured Overnight Financing Rate) +350bp and neither facility has any prepayment penalties.

data.title: ‘Extreme’ AI spending could destroy capital – Greenlight Capital’s David Einhorn
data.description:
“Extreme” and unprecedented AI spending could cause capital destruction in the current economic cycle, according to Greenlight Capital founder and president David Einhorn.

“The numbers that are being thrown around are so extreme that it’s really, really hard to understand them,” he said, during a panel discussion hosted by Simplify Asset Management at the New York Stock Exchange Thursday evening.

The hedge fund manager added that the unprecedented amount of “extreme” spending on AI infrastructure by giants such as Apple (APPL), Meta Platforms (META), an OpenAI could be tied to a “reasonable chance that a tremendous amount of capital destruction is going to come through this cycle.”

Even if AI technology proves to be transformative, surpassing analysts’ bullish forecasts, returns are highly uncertain, he said, and questioned whether spending billions or trillions of dollars a year will be worth the investment.

Recently, Nvidia (NVDA) announced plans to invest $100B in OpenAI to build data centers. In addition, Nebius Group (NBIS) signed an agreement with Microsoft (MSFT) for $17.4B in AI infrastructure over the next five years. Alphabet (GOOGL), (GOOG) is also planning to invest $9B AI and cloud initiatives through 2026.

Einhorn said while many projects will be built, investors may not see the expected payoffs.

He also spoke about weak job growth. “I’m a little bit more of the view that we’re heading into or have been in a recession,” he said. “Jobs aren’t growing, and the workweek is shrinking, and productivity is not that great.”

data.title: Macerich upgraded to Buy at BofA on higher growth, path to lower leverage
data.description:
Bank of America Securities upgraded Macerich (NYSE:MAC) to Buy from Neutral on Friday, citing the shopping mall REIT's improved portfolio composition, stronger balance sheet, and solid leasing progress.

Analyst Samir Khanal sees strong net operating income growth in 2026-28 and beyond driven by lease-up, signed not opened commencement, major capital projects, and other incremental redevelopments.

"We expect NOI and FFO growth to inflect in mid-26 and continue accelerating in '27 and '28," Khanal wrote in a note to clients. BofA's '28 FFO estimate of $1.99 is at the top end of MAC's $1.73-2.05 target, including $0.08 accretion from its Crabtree Mall acquisition.

The analyst also noted that Macerich (NYSE:MAC) has reduced net debt/EBITDA ~0.8x to 7.9x since it started its Path Forward initiatives. He sees a path to reducing that ratio to the mid-6x area by the end of 2028, which should warrant an AFFO multiple expansion.

BofA's Buy rating on Macerich (NYSE:MAC) aligns with the average Wall Street rating of Buy and contrasts with the SA Quant rating of Sell.

data.title: Apellis down as Goldman Sachs cuts to sell on risks to outlook
data.description:
Apellis Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:APLS) traded lower on Friday after Goldman Sachs downgraded the company to Sell from Neutral, citing risks to its outlook and handing the eye drug developer its only bearish rating on Wall Street.

Analyst Salveen Richter highlighted the long-term growth prospects for the company’s main product, Syfovre, a treatment approved in the U.S. for the eye condition, geographic atrophy.

However, Richter also listed potential risks to the company’s outlook, including competition from Astella Pharma’s (OTCPK:ALPMF) (OTCPK:ALPMY) rival GA therapy Izervay, which, according to experts, has a better risk-benefit profile, she stated.

Other risks, including limited market penetration of GA therapies and high patient attrition, have prompted Richter to predict $1.1B in peak sales for the company in 2030, lower than the $1.3B in the consensus.

The analyst is optimistic over the potential of Apellis’ (NASDAQ:APLS) experimental therapy Empaveli against rare kidney diseases such as C3 glomerulopathy, given its superior data and labeling compared to rival drug Fabhalta from Novartis (NVS).

However, with a Street low price target of $18 on the stock, Richter argued that Apellis' (NASDAQ:APLS) share price already reflects the opportunity for Empaveli.

data.title: Pop Culture Group shares sink on 5M stock offering
data.description:
• Pop Culture (NASDAQ:CPOP) shares are in a freefall Friday after the company said it would sell 5M class A shares to institutional investors, diluting the holdings of existing shareholders.
  • The shares will be sold at $1.20 per share, representing a discount of 40% to Thursday’s closing price of Pop Culture (NASDAQ:CPOP) stock.
  • The transaction will provide the company with gross proceeds of $6M to be used for general corporate purposes.
  • The negative reaction wiped out all gains achieved on Thursday when the company  announced plans to acquire an additional 1,000 Bitcoin over the course of the next year to fund its $100M Crypto Pop Fund.
  • Shares last traded at a loss of more than 53%.
